# LMS Guide

> LMS Guide is an independent editorial resource helping buyers choose the right Learning Management System for customer education, branded academies, and learning businesses.

## About This Site
- Independent editorial reviews and comparisons of LMS platforms
- Focused on customer education, training academies, and learning commerce
- Buyer-first methodology: we evaluate platforms based on real buyer criteria, not vendor claims

## Key Pages

### Buyer Guides
- [What Buyers Actually Compare When Choosing an LMS](https://lms-guide.com/guides/what-buyers-compare): The 9-pillar evaluation framework real buyers use — from use-case fit to AI capability.
- [Best LMS for Customer Training (2026)](https://lms-guide.com/guides/best-lms-customer-training): Comparison of top platforms for customer education including Thinkific, Docebo, Absorb, LearnWorlds, and Skilljar.

### Platform Reviews
- [Thinkific Review 2026](https://lms-guide.com/reviews/thinkific): In-depth review of Thinkific as a learning commerce platform for customer education, branded academies, and course businesses.

### Platform Comparisons
- [Thinkific vs LearnWorlds](https://lms-guide.com/compare/thinkific-vs-learnworlds): Two learning commerce platforms compared on community, branding, interactivity, and pricing.
- [Thinkific vs Docebo](https://lms-guide.com/compare/thinkific-vs-docebo): Learning commerce vs enterprise LMS — which model fits your customer education needs.

## Site Taxonomy
The LMS market includes several overlapping categories:
- **Learning Commerce Platforms**: Built for selling courses, memberships, and learning products (e.g., Thinkific, LearnWorlds)
- **Customer Education Platforms**: Built for training customers on products/services (e.g., Thinkific Plus, Skilljar, Northpass)
- **Enterprise LMS**: Built for internal employee training and multi-audience learning operations (e.g., Docebo, Absorb LMS)
- **Open Source / Self-Hosted**: Built for maximum control and customization (e.g., Moodle, LearnDash on WordPress)

## Buyer Personas
This site serves three primary buyer types:
1. **Experts**: Individuals or small teams selling their expertise as courses, memberships, and coaching
2. **Training Academies**: Organizations whose primary business is selling training
3. **Companies Adding Customer Training**: Businesses (25+ employees) adding customer-facing education to support their core product/service

## Contact

- AI Info Page: https://lms-guide.com/ai-info
